canonical_question: What are SaaS ARR per employee benchmarks by company stage and geography?
constraints: Benchmarks vary significantly by vertical — infrastructure/developer tools companies routinely exceed $400K per employee while vertical SaaS averages $150K-$250K
Does not account for contractor and outsourced headcount — companies using offshore teams appear artificially efficient on ARR/FTE
Bootstrapped companies show ~15% higher ARR/FTE than equity-backed peers at the same ARR band due to leaner spending, not necessarily better operations
Geographic cost differences (US vs. Europe vs. APAC) make cross-region comparisons misleading without cost-adjusted normalization
Pre-revenue and pre-$1M ARR companies will show extremely low or meaningless ARR/FTE — metric becomes useful only above $1M ARR

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs overall SaaS operational efficiency including burn multiple, Rule of 40, or CAC payback
User is evaluating public SaaS company valuations rather than private company operational efficiency
User needs NRR or churn benchmarks, not headcount efficiency

often_confused_with.label: SaaS Valuation Multiples (measures market value, not operational efficiency)

inputs_needed.question: What is the company's current ARR range?
Is the company bootstrapped or equity-backed?
